title: fagraea tacapala subsp. tacapala
intro: Fagraea tacapala subsp. tacapala, also known as Tacapala or Tacamahacahua, is a flowering plant species belonging to the family Gentianaceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of indigestion and nausea. The Tacapala plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to harness its health benefits and medicinal properties.
